reasoning_chain_text: 1. For Washington agency contracts in fiscal year 2017, identify which candidate firms rank in the top 20 by contract count.
2. For fiscal year 2018, keep the candidate firms that again rank in the top 20 by contract count.
3. For fiscal year 2019, keep the candidate firms that again rank in the top 20 by contract count.
4. For fiscal year 2020, intersect the four annual contractor lists to isolate the firms that rank in the top 20 in every year.
5. On one recurring engineering-firm page, record the firm's founding year and home city.
6. On a second recurring engineering-firm page, record the firm's founding year and home city.
7. On the remaining recurring engineering-firm page, record the firm's founding year and home city.
8. Using the active construction manager projects data, keep the recurring firms that currently have no New York State construction manager projects.

9. On the home-city page for the firm that survives the founding-year and project filters, identify the Native American interpreter involved in the land-cession negotiations tied to that city's founding.
10. On the interpreter biography page for that negotiator, record the requested birth year.
original_final_question: Consider the engineering firm founded after 1914 that has no current NY State construction manager projects, yet consistently ranked in the top 20 Washington State contractors by contract count annually from 2017 to 2020. In what year was the Native American interpreter born who helped negotiate the land cession that led to the founding of the city where that firm is based? Please return the year only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: Node 1: Top 20 contractors by contract count in WA State FY 2017 -> includes HDR, Jacobs, HNTB
Node 2: Top 20 contractors by contract count in WA State FY 2018 -> includes HDR, Jacobs, HNTB
Node 3: Top 20 contractors by contract count in WA State FY 2019 -> includes HDR, Jacobs, HNTB
Node 4: Top 20 contractors by contract count in WA State FY 2020 -> includes HDR, Jacobs, HNTB
Intersection of nodes 1-4: Contractors in top 20 all four years -> {Jacobs Engineering Group, HNTB Corporation, HDR Engineering}
Node 5: HDR is based in Omaha and started in 1917
Node 6: Jacobs Engineering was founded in 1947
Node 7: HNTB Corporation was founded in 1914
Node 8: Zero NY State construction manager projects -> {HDR, HNTB}
Node 9: Omaha land-cession negotiations identify interpreter Logan Fontenelle
Node 10: Logan Fontenelle was born in 1825
